records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Impaled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Impaling.]

defs: To pierce with a pale; to put to death by fixing on a sharp stake. See Empale.
quotes: Then with what life remains, impaled, and left To writhe at leisure round the bloody stake.

defs: To inclose, as with pales or stakes; to surround.
quotes: Impale him with your weapons round about.

defs: To join, as two coats of arms on one shield, palewise; hence, to join in honorable mention.
quotes: Ordered the admission of St. Patrick to the same to be matched and impaled with the blessed Virgin in the honor thereof.
